title: JJThunder Hell Is Fire
description: Terraforms Nether into a massive 2032 height world
body:
<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/5obIGMUUyyc" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

# Introduction:
JJThunder Hell is Fire, is a datapack that terraforms the nether into 2032 block world, allowing it to contain all 3 world generation types:

- Caves: Cave-like terrain that occupies the bottom-half of the dimension
- Overlands: Overworld-like terrain that starts at around y=1000
- Floating islands= End-like terrain that starts at y=1500

# Notes:
- Allocate more RAM depending on your render distance.
- Do not use the data pack in an existing world as it will destroy it.
- Not compatible with other world generation data packs or mods derived from data packs like incedium.
- Compatible with world generation mods like better nether, byg, regions unexplored with limitations (Not Tested, so please give it a try and validate if needed).
- Donwloading performance mods is highly recommended, as any world height increase world generation datapack reduces performance
- Ore generation remains untouched. Please let me know if ores only generate till y=128 only.

#  FYI:
- Floating islands contain unique and massive caverns different from bottom caves, so don't hesitate to explore.
- Must use shaders to unlock its full potential. I myself use BSL shaders and render fog on a further distance without completely eliminating them.
- No photos are taken for now. However, if any videos are released featuring this data pack, I will feature them here. (Only ones that are well done will be featured)
- I have included the datapack for all versions ranging from 1.19 to 1.21, however, backporting to earlier versions isn't possible.
- This is a 1st iteration of the data pack, so feedback may be needed.
